intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Animated state changes between dashboard views create a sense of continuous flow and reduce cognitive friction
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: User values motion as a connector—treat navigation, data updates, and metric reveals as choreographed sequences rather than instant jumps
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immediacy and clarity—eliminate decorative motion, show results instantly, let static layouts do the talking

evidence.hypothesis: Animated state changes between dashboard views create a sense of continuous flow and reduce cognitive friction
evidence.implication: User values motion as a connector—treat navigation, data updates, and metric reveals as choreographed sequences rather than instant jumps

facade.hypothesis: Users want prominent metric displays but without supporting narrative or comparison
facade.axisTargeted: Charts vs Text — whether key metrics should stand alone or embedded in explanatory context
facade.label: Big numbers, small context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard favors bold, isolated KPIs (weight, steps, sleep hours) as primary visual anchors with minimal annotation
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard embeds metrics within sentences, trends, or interpretive frames that tell a story rather than just show the number

facade.hypothesis: Users building personal health dashboards prefer approachable, conversational language over clinical or technical jargon—this feels less intimidating and more motivating for habit tracking.
facade.label: Casual tone, friendly language
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, conversational copy. Use 'you' and personal encouragement. Name things colloquially (e.g., 'sleep wins' not 'nocturnal duration metrics').
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward clear, neutral, slightly formal language. Let data and structure do the talking. Reserve personality for highlights only.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer navigation split between a persistent left sidebar (core sections) and tab-based filtering (experiment parameters) rather than consolidated into one navigation layer
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation hierarchy — sidebar dominance vs. distributed nav patterns
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs below
facade.acceptImplies: Explore more sidebar-primary layouts; test how much content can live in persistent left column before crowding occurs
facade.rejectImplies: Investigate tab-only or bottom nav approaches; test whether collapsible sidebar or top-level tabs feel less cluttered for health tracking workflows

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard works best with clean, uncluttered data visualization rather than colorful density—user needs to see trends at a glance without visual noise
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers clarity and restraint in visual design; favors sparse, purposeful elements; likely wants a calm, focused experience
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer richer visual feedback, more color or dimensionality in data display, or finds stark minimalism cold; might want more immediate visual reward or ambient data presence

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers approachable, gentle interfaces over sharp geometric design — important for a health dashboard that should feel supportive rather than clinical or sterile
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Favor organic, curved UI elements; avoid hard-edged card layouts and stark borders. Health tracking should feel like a trusted companion, not a medical instrument.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, modern minimalism with distinct edges and geometric clarity. Sharp divisions between data sections could feel more organized and less 'touchy-feely'.

facade.hypothesis: Allowing metric values to animate and refresh while the user is actively scrolling through the dashboard maintains momentum and rewards continuous exploration rather than requiring pauses
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity — whether animations persist during user interaction or pause for clarity
facade.label: Data updates mid-scroll
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace overlapping, non-blocking animations that layer with user gestures; treat data refresh as a visual event that compounds momentum
facade.rejectImplies: Pause all animations during scroll; prioritize content legibility over motion; use instant updates that don't compete with user input
